query: i finally got claude to figure out my whole month-end close in quickbooks — exporting the reports, reconciling, emailing the summary. it works now. can you write it up so next month a fresh session just runs it without poking around again?
every monday i have claude pull our stripe payouts and drop them in a sheet, but it re-figures-out the same thing every single week. how do i make it just replay the exact steps instead?
my automated invoice script worked for 3 months then broke when the vendor portal changed their button layout. how should i have built it so it doesn't shatter every time their UI moves?
should i even bother turning this into a permanent automation? i only need to scrape this competitor's pricing maybe once a quarter and their site changes a lot

i have a runbook doc a fresh claude session is supposed to execute end to end. today it ran but quietly produced an empty file and nobody noticed until a client complained. how do i make the design catch that and run safely?
which parts of this data-pipeline task should i hardcode into a script vs leave the agent to figure out at runtime? some steps are stable and some depend on the input
turn this exploration into a durable design — pre-write all the code and the workflow so there's zero ambiguity when a clean session executes it later
i want my deploy checklist to be replayable by an agent with preconditions and a rollback if a step fails, and it should bail out if the environment isn't what it expects
claude keeps re-discovering the same API quirks on every run. i want to capture the working approach once so it stops re-exploring and just executes
how do i make an agent automation that fails loud instead of silently when the thing it depends on drifts out from under it?
i have 5 pipeline stages handing data to each other and i need to define the typed contract between each stage and decide which agent owns which decision
harden this one classification prompt's output — the label field keeps coming back plausible but wrong, i need a membership test on it
this prompt is full of vague words like 'summarize nicely' and 'handle appropriately' — make the instructions mechanical and checkable
review my skill against the captured standard and log defects with severities, but don't fix anything
write me a python script that moves files from my Downloads folder into subfolders by file extension
how do i set up a cron job to run my database backup at 2am every night?
design the DAG for my multi-agent research system — how many subagents should the orchestrator spawn for a comparison task?
my terraform keeps showing configuration drift in the console, how do i reconcile it back to the IaC state?
what's the actual difference between a workflow and an agent in anthropic's framing?
make this onboarding SOP document less ambiguous so my human team follows it the same way every time
